'''''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Nights of Anger''''' is the fifth story-arc of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] series of comics.

==Plot summary==
On Arkania, whose society is in a state of segregation between pure Arkanians and offshoots, Jarael disguises herself as a pure specimen to get an analysis of Camper's blood. However, this move attracts the attention of [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania|Adascorp]], which recognizes Camper as one of its former employees; as such, the ''Last Resort'' is captured by the Adascorp flagship, the ''[[Arkanian Legacy]]'',<ref name="KOTOR 16" /> and Jarael and Camper are brought before [[Arkoh Adasca|Arkoh, Lord Adasca]]. After Rohlan's unsuccessful attempt to free Jarael, she is kept close to Adasca to ensure that Camper finishes the secret project he used to work on before fleeing and hiding on Taris.<ref name="KOTOR 17" />

It is aboard the ''Arkanian Legacy'' that Jarael learns about the past life of "Camper", actually named [[Gorman Vandrayk]]. His past research has attracted Adascorp's attention to giant space slugs, or [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorths]], which it intended to use as weapons of planetary destruction. As Vandrayk has now finished his debt to the corporation, giving it full control over the exogorths, Adasca intends to use the newfound weapons as means of political pressure.<ref name="KOTOR 18">[[Knights of the Old Republic 18|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 18]]</ref>

Meanwhile, ''Courageous'' itself is boarded and destroyed by [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]]. Admiral Karath, Lieutenant Onasi and Karath's second-in-command [[Dallan Morvis]] are left with no other choice but to evacuate through Zayne's prison cell, having to rely on him to get to the cargo bay and escape on Carth's ship, the ''[[Deadweight]]''.<ref name="KOTOR 17" />
